summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that sending a malformed rechargingInfo parameter without an underscore causes a panic in the CHF service, demonstrating the denial of service vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: Sending a PUT request to /nchf-convergedcharging/v3/recharging/nounderScoreHere should cause the service to panic with 'index out of range' error, crashing the HTTP handler.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched the CHF service, sent a malformed PUT request to the vulnerable endpoint, and the service panicked with 'runtime error: index out of range [1] with length 1' when processing the request.
verification_method: The PoC sends a curl request with a malformed parameter and captures the panic in the service logs. Success is verified by checking that the panic message appears in the output, confirming the crash.
evidence_produced_by_run: Service started and listening on HTTP port
Malformed PUT request sent with parameter 'malicious_value' (no underscore)
Service panic logged: runtime error: index out of range [1] with length 1
Stack trace points to internal/sbi/api_convergedcharging.go line 178
Service handler crashed, demonstrating denial of service

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the local repo directory mounted into the container at /target-repo.
